findings.description: Cover page image/layout is missing - the PDF has a styled title page with centered title and author information, while the HTML renders it as plain text without the cover page styling
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows a formal cover page with '2019 White Lake Aquatic Vegetation Survey' centered with 'Survey Conducted by: North Carolina State University'. HTML page 1 does not replicate this as a separate cover page.

findings.description: Table 1 in PDF is rendered as a compact formatted table, while HTML renders it as a full-width styled table. Both contain the same data, but the text mentions the body text references 'Dwarf milfoil was found at 40 points in 2018' while Table 2 shows 40 and Figure 9 caption says 30 points - this is a discrepancy in the source, but the HTML faithfully reproduces it.
findings.evidence: PDF Table 2 Dwarf Milfoil 2018 = 40, Figure 9 caption says 30 points. Same inconsistency in HTML.

findings.description: Figure 9 caption discrepancy: PDF shows 'Dwarf milfoil was found at 30 sample points in 2018' but the text body says '40 points in 2018'. The HTML faithfully reproduces both the figure caption (30) and the body text (40). This is a source document inconsistency reflected in both.
findings.evidence: PDF Figure 9 caption: '30 sample points in 2018'; body text: '40 points in 2018'. HTML same.

findings.description: The text body states Dwarf milfoil was found at '40 points in 2018' but Figure 9 caption on page 7 says '30 sample points in 2018'. The HTML reproduces both the body text (40) and the figure caption (30), matching the PDF's own internal inconsistency. However, Table 2 in the PDF shows Dwarf Milfoil at 40 for 2018, so the Figure 9 caption '30' appears to be an error in the source PDF. The HTML faithfully reproduces this inconsistency.
findings.evidence: PDF page 2: 'Dwarf milfoil (Myriophyllum tentellum) was found at 40 points in 2018 (Figure 9)'; PDF page 7 Figure 9 caption: 'Dwarf milfoil was found at 30 sample points in 2018'; Table 2 shows 40 for 2018. HTML reproduces all three values as-is.

findings.description: Scientific name for Hydrilla is misspelled in the PDF body text as 'Hydrilla verticillata' but the HTML renders it without italics. More importantly, the PDF text on page 2 spells it 'verticillata' (correct spelling) while the OCR/text shows 'verticillata'. The HTML drops the italic formatting for all scientific names (Hydrilla verticillata, Fontinalis antipyretica, Myriophyllum humile, Eleocharis baldwinii, Myriophyllum tentellum).
findings.evidence: PDF uses italic formatting for scientific names throughout page 2. HTML renders all scientific names in plain text without italic tags.

findings.description: The document has a nested <main> element (main inside main), which is an HTML structural issue but does not affect content fidelity.
The grid pattern is described as '5 ¾ acre' in the PDF but the HTML also uses '5 ¾ acre' — this is correct and consistent.
findings.evidence: Both PDF and HTML: '5 ¾ acre grid pattern'
